name: Castillo de Caracuel by Almodóvar del Campo — circular by Cabezarados
name_original: Castillo de Caracuel por Almodóvar del Campo — circular por Cabezarados
description: This moderate 38.5-mile road cycling route near Almodóvar del Campo features the historic Castillo de Caracuel and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Road cycling around Almodóvar Del Campo features a varied landscape, encompassing the Sierra Madrona mountain range and the Valle de Alcudia Natural Park. The region is characterized by rolling hills, open plains, and areas of Mediterranean forest, offering diverse terrain for road cyclists. Routes often include ascents into the Sierra Madrona, providing elevation changes, alongside flatter sections through agricultural lands and valleys. Best road cycling routes around Almodóvar Del Campo The most popular road cycling route is Puerto Madrona from Puertollano – circular via Mestanza and the Sierra Madrona, a 72.2 miles (116.2 km) trail that takes 6 hours 16 minutes to complete. This difficult route includes significant climbs through the Sierra Madrona. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillo de Caracuel by Almodóvar del Campo — circular by Cabezarados, a moderate 38.5 miles (61.9 km) path. This route offers views of the historic Castillo de Caracuel and surrounding countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Almodóvar del Campo y Argamasilla de Calatrava desde Brazatortas – circular por el Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona, a 42.8 miles (68.9 km) trail leading through the Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia and Sierra Madrona, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Almodóvar Del Campo is defined by routes through the Sierra Madrona, the Valle de Alcudia Natural Park, and historical sites. The network offers options for easy road bike trails as well as challenging road cycling routes. The routes in Almodóvar Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Almodóvar Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes in and around Almodóvar Del Campo, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: Yes, Almodóvar Del Campo offers 7 easy road cycling routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler terrain and shorter distances.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 12 difficult road cycling routes. Many of these routes include significant climbs through the Sierra Madrona, such as the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084847686/puerto-madrona-from-puertollano-circular-via-mestanza-and-the-sierra-madrona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto Madrona from Puertollano – circular via Mestanza and the Sierra Madrona&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 2,300 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes around Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1251056031/castillo-de-caracuel-by-almodovar-del-campo-circular-by-cabezarados" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Caracuel by Almodóvar del Campo — circular by Cabezarados&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of the historic castle, and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1084926712/almodovar-del-campo-y-argamasilla-de-calatrava-desde-brazatortas-circular-por-el-parque-natural-del-valle-de-alcudia-y-sierra-madrona" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar del Campo y Argamasilla de Calatrava desde Brazatortas – circular por el Parque Natural del Valle de Alcudia y Sierra Madrona&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some longer distance road cycling routes in the Almodóvar Del Campo region?
A: For those looking for longer rides, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1085143036/almaden-from-veredas-brazatortas-circular-by-roman-ruins-of-sisapo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén from Veredas-Brazatortas – circular by Roman ruins of Sisapo&lt;/a&gt; cover nearly 130 kilometers, offering extensive exploration of the area's landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: Road cycling in Almodóvar Del Campo offers diverse scenery, from the rolling hills and open plains of agricultural lands to the more rugged terrain of the Sierra Madrona mountain range and the Mediterranean forests within the Valle de Alcudia Natural Park. You'll encounter a mix of natural beauty and historical elements.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in points of interest. You might cycle past natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maar and Strombolian volcano of Almodóvar del Campo&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6470149" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de Ventillas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta de la Villa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6295622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar Mill&lt;/a&gt; also dot the landscape.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, which includes both challenging mountain passes and scenic routes through natural parks, as well as the opportunity to explore historical sites and natural monuments.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: The spring and autumn months (April-May and September-October) are generally ideal for road cycling in Almodóvar Del Campo. The weather is typically milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler temperatures of winter, making for more comfortable rides.

Q: Are there routes that pass through towns or villages where I can find cafes or refreshments?
A: Many routes in the Almodóvar Del Campo area connect various towns and villages, such as Puertollano, Mestanza, and Argamasilla de Calatrava. These settlements typically offer opportunities to stop for refreshments, making it easy to refuel during your ride. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086273217/almodovar-del-campo-and-abeno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar del Campo and Abenó&lt;/a&gt; route passes through several populated areas.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that are less crowded in Almodóvar Del Campo?
A: While popular routes can see more traffic, the region's extensive network of over 40 routes means it's often possible to find quieter roads, especially on weekdays or by exploring some of the less-trafficked moderate and difficult routes that venture deeper into the natural parks and rural areas.
tips:
In the Alcudia Valley, we find the municipality of Almodóvar del Campo built on the slope of a volcano, so present in Campo de Calatrava, and whose crater is now a lagoon. The Arab footprint is the most emblematic of the city, leaving behind the memory of the Castle and above all the unique Parish of Our Lady of the Assumption. There you can see the first videomapping in Spain permanently installed in a closed space, under the theme of the life and work of San Juan de Ávila.

Monuments and centers of cultural interest are scattered throughout its streets: the Palermo Museum, the Casa-Convento de La Merced, the current home of the Hieronymite nuns, modernist in style and dating from the 19th century. Continuing the visit you can visit the Cervantina Library, inside which an infinite number of editions of Don Quixote are collected, in different languages, formats and periods, as well as canvases and drawings related to the Cervantine character. From here you can also see the Municipal Theatre, built in 1845 on the site of the old Carmelite Convent.

An initiative of the Junta de Castilla - La Mancha and the Laborvalía association has set itself the goal of decorating ten old grain silos with murals.

In this case, it is a work by the artist Okuda San Miguel, who, inspired by the province of La Mancha and the "Almodóvar girls", has incorporated some characters from the films of the La Mancha film director, being able to see Rossy de Palma or the poster of the movie "Bad Education".

It was the place where one of the oldest parcels in Campo de Calatrava, Caracuel, was founded in 1170. Even so, a few years later it was recovered by the Muslims until the victory of Alfonso VIII in Las Navas de Tolosa in 1212.

Mainly, the pentagonal tower of the castle stands out, which covers an older one, from the Almohad period. Today, remains of what was its square enclosure and even the remains of a cistern are preserved. We can access the castle through a narrow stone path that begins behind the Hermitage of Santo Cristo de Caracuel de Calatrava.
Section of the CM-4115 road through the Dehesa Labrada, good track and beautiful scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Castillo de Caracuel circular route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is classified as moderate. It's largely on well-paved surfaces with a relatively gentle gradient, but some segments may require good fitness. The total elevation gain is around 286 meters over nearly 62 kilometers, making for manageable climbs and descents.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Almodóvar del Campo, being the starting point, would be the most logical place to find parking. Look for public parking options within the town to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of historical and rural sights. Key highlights include the historic Castillo de Caracuel, the Parish Church of Our Lady of the Assumption in Almodóvar del Campo, and the Silo (Corral Clva). You'll also pass through diverse landscapes, including farmlands and woodlands.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Castile-La Mancha generally experiences hot summers and cold winters. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are typically the most pleasant seasons for cycling, offering milder temperatures and comfortable conditions for a moderate ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Campo de Calatrava region?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are generally required for cycling on public roads and trails in the Campo de Calatrava region, including this route. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is the Castillo de Caracuel circular cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is primarily a road cycling route. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, bringing a dog on a racebike ride of this distance and duration might not be ideal for the animal. If you choose to bring a dog, ensure it is well-trained, can keep pace safely, and that you adhere to all local leash laws and waste disposal regulations.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other official trails. You'll find it crosses parts of the Cañada Real Segoviana, Los Pozuelos de Calatrava - La Viñuela, and Camino de Ciudad Real.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by largely well-paved surfaces, making it suitable for racebikes. You can expect a mix of gentle ascents and descents, with no overly challenging unpaved sections or extreme elevation changes that would require pushing your bike.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners looking to get into road cycling?
faqs.answer: While classified as moderate, this 61.9 km route with nearly 300 meters of elevation gain might be a significant challenge for absolute beginners. It's best suited for cyclists with a good level of fitness and some experience with longer road rides. Beginners might consider shorter, flatter routes first.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route or in nearby towns?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near Almodóvar del Campo and Cabezarados. Both towns offer amenities where you can find cafes, restaurants, and places to refuel. It's always a good idea to check opening hours, especially in smaller villages, and carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: What is an insider tip for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: For a rewarding experience, consider taking a short break at the Castillo de Caracuel. While the route itself is for cycling, there's a 'decent path' leading up to the ruined castle from a nearby hermitage. This allows for a brief exploration of the historical landmark, adding a cultural dimension to your ride.
